title: Key stage 2 tests: making adaptations to modified large print test materials
body: "MLP versions of the KS2 tests are available electronically as adaptable Microsoft Word documents in the following formats:   18 point, A4 portrait 24 point, A4 portrait 36 point, A3 portrait 48 point, A3 landscape   Past test materials   Schools should ensure that pupils who will use the modified tests are familiar with the MLP format before taking the tests.    Past test materials in adaptable MLP formats are available only from the 2025 tests onwards, as this is when they were piloted.    You will find them in our collection of past test materials.  Early opening to adapt a test paper   If you believe you will require more than one hour to make complex adaptations, you must apply for early opening.    You can find out more about this in the KS2 access arrangements guidance.  Making further adaptations to the tests   Please check any adaptations carefully to ensure no changes have been made that could invalidate the concept being assessed in the question. We cannot compensate, or give special consideration, where test papers have been incorrectly adapted.   You must ensure that the accuracy of the materials is not affected. If you adapt the corresponding text, questions or page references incorrectly, pupils may be disadvantaged.    There may be questions in the mathematics tests that are unsuitable for further enlargement. If that is the case, the modified test administration instructions we delivered with your printed test papers will provide guidance.  Permitted adaptations   You are allowed to:     change the page orientation    change emphasis – for example, underline can change to bold    break booklets into sections to help with administration    make the quotation marks non-directional    add the number of marks a question is worth – this should usually be placed immediately after the text of the question for modified tests    repeat equations or phrases to avoid the need to turn the page    print the test materials on different coloured paper    change the font used to one used in everyday classroom practice    create a screen-ready version for use with assisted reading devices where these are allowed – please note that screen readers are not permitted in the English reading test    adjust line spacing    make booklets one question per page    adjust the area for answer or answer lines, such as increasing line weight, or replacing with a suitably sized box     You are not allowed to:     increase the size of diagrams in mathematics questions relating to measurement – these are aligned to the mark schemes at the length they are presented in the MLP papers    change the order or the wording of the questions    Important points to note     Ensure that you have all the questions, that they are in the correct order and that the page numbers are correct.    Ensure that all words that should be enlarged or bold for emphasis are modified to at least 6 point sizes larger than the pupil’s modified font size.    Ensure that all tables, grids and boxes are modified with thicker borders and enlarged, if necessary, due to the increase in the modified text size.    Ensure that...

taxons.title: Support for special educational needs and disability (SEND)
